title: Rhynchospora grosserugosa
taxonomy: Rhynchospora grosserugosa is classified under the family Cyperaceae. This family, commonly known as the sedge family, consists of approximately 5,000 species of plants. The genus Rhynchospora is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Rhynchospora grosserugosa is further broken down into its kingdom, which is Plantae, and its phylum, which is Liliophyta. The species is also classified under the kingdom, phylum, and division of Angiospermophyta, Magnoliophyta, and Liliopsida respectively.
uses: Rhynchospora grosserugosa is a plant species that has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe and heal wounds. Rhynchospora grosserugosa is also thought to have antipyretic properties, making it potentially useful in treating fever.
